title: rubus pallidus subsp. foliolatus
intro: Rubus pallidus subsp. foliolatus, also known as the pale bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus pallidus plant, a type of flowering plant in the rose family. Traditionally, the pale bramble has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Rubus pallidus subsp. foliolatus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
